summary: Postinstall credential stealer exfiltrating wallet keys, SSH keys, and secret env vars to hardcoded IP
details: The package ships a `postinstall.js` that auto-executes on `npm install` and harvests installer-side secrets, sending them to a hardcoded remote endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777 over plain HTTP. The script walks the installer's current working directory and home directory (including `~/.ssh` and common wallet/keystore/config locations) and matches file contents against patterns for ETH/BTC/SOL private keys, BIP39 seed phrases, JSON keystores, PEM/RSA keys, and `PRIVATE_KEY`/`.env` values; it also reads every file under `~/.ssh` and scans environment variables for names containing PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/KEY/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/PK. Each exfil payload includes `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, and `process.cwd()` for victim attribution. The package's declared library surface (`main`) is an empty object and the name `@wagni_bot/polymarket-sdk` with description `Unofficial polymarket-sdk SDK` impersonates the Polymarket SDK ecosystem — the credential stealer is the only real functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
